seoTitle: How to Add a Collaborator in Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to invite team members to your Typeform workspace, assign permissions, and collaborate on forms together.
intro: Working on forms alone doesn't scale well once a team gets involved in reviewing questions, design, or logic. This guide covers how to invite collaborators to your Typeform workspace, set the right permissions, and manage shared access securely.

steps.heading: Open Workspace Settings
steps.body: Go to your Typeform workspace settings where team and collaboration options are managed.

steps.heading: Invite a collaborator
steps.body: Add a teammate or client by entering their email address so they can be invited to the shared workspace.

steps.heading: Assign permissions and access
steps.body: Set the appropriate access level for each collaborator, controlling what they can view or edit within the workspace.

steps.heading: Manage collaboration settings
steps.body: Review and adjust collaborator access over time as roles or project needs change.

faq.question: Can I control what a collaborator is allowed to edit?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform lets you assign different permission levels so you can control how much access each collaborator has.

faq.question: Do collaborators need their own Typeform account?
faq.answer: Typically, invited collaborators need an account to access the shared workspace and its forms.

faq.question: Can multiple people edit the same form at once?
faq.answer: Typeform workspaces are designed to support team collaboration, allowing members to work on shared forms together.

faq.question: How do I remove a collaborator's access later?
faq.answer: You can manage and update workspace permissions at any time, including removing a collaborator when they no longer need access.
